entities.name: PENINSULAR INDIA
entities.description: The geographic region in India where the case study on varietal turnover is conducted

entities.name: ASIA
entities.description: The continent where the Green Revolution led to a rapid increase in food production

entities.name: SOUTH ASIA
entities.description: The region where poverty is concentrated and dryland crops are significant

entities.name: CROP GENETIC IMPROVEMENT
entities.description: Research focused on improving the genetic makeup of crops to increase productivity and reduce poverty

entities.name: GREEN REVOLUTION
entities.description: A period of rapid agricultural transformation and increased food production in Asia

entities.name: FOOD PRODUCTION
entities.description: The process of generating food crops, which increased significantly during the Green Revolution

entities.name: POVERTY
entities.description: A socio-economic state that crop genetic improvement and increased food production aim to reduce

entities.name: YIELD
entities.description: The measure of crop productivity that genetic improvement seeks to increase or stabilize

entities.name: DRYLAND CROPS
entities.description: Crops grown in arid or semi-arid regions, particularly in South Asia

entities.name: VARIETAL CHANGE
entities.description: The process of replacing old crop varieties with improved ones

entities.name: CROP MANAGEMENT PRACTICES
entities.description: Agricultural techniques used to optimize crop growth, often adopted alongside new varieties
